ddls:
CREATE TABLE "rim_specifications" (
  "rim_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rim */,
  "nb" INTEGER /* Number of bolts */,
  "od" REAL /* Outer diameter */,
  "thickness" REAL /* Thickness */,
  "hub_x" REAL /* Hub dimension X */,
  "hub_a" REAL /* Hub dimension A */,
  "wn_thk" REAL /* Wheel nut thickness */,
  "rf_dia" REAL /* Rim flange diameter */,
  "rf_height" REAL /* Rim flange height */,
  "pcd" REAL /* Pitch circle diameter */,
  "no_of_bolts" INTEGER /* Number of bolts */,
  "bolt_size_unc" TEXT /* Bolt size UNC */,
  "rf_sb_length" REAL /* Rim flange stud bolt length */,
  "hole_size" TEXT /* Hole size */,
  "iso_sb_size" TEXT /* ISO stud bolt size */,
  "wn_kg" REAL /* Wheel nut weight */,
  "bolts_wt_rf" REAL /* Bolts weight in rim flange */,
  "weld_flat" REAL /* Weld flat size */,
  "full_hei" REAL /* Full height */,
  "neck_degree" REAL /* Neck degree */,
  "sch" REAL /* Schedule number */,
  "design_name" TEXT /* Design name of the rim */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the rim */,
  "material_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the material used */,
  "production_cost" REAL /* Production cost of the rim */,
  "selling_price" REAL /* Selling price of the rim */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rim_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rim_specifications_material_id FOREIGN KEY ("material_id") REFERENCES material_inventory ("material_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "material_inventory" (
  "material_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each material */,
  "material_name" TEXT /* Name of the material */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of material (e.g., steel, welding material) */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Current quantity in inventory */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measure (e.g., kg, meters) */,
  "reorder_threshold" INTEGER /* Threshold quantity for reorder */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the supplier */,
  "lead_time" INTEGER /* Lead time for material delivery */,
  "unit_cost" REAL /* Cost per unit of the material */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("material_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_material_inventory_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suppliers" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "supplier_name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the supplier */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating of the supplier */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the supplier */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the supplier */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_schedule" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production schedule entry */,
  "rim_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the rim being produced */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the production run */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the production run */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of rims to be produced */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the production run (e.g., scheduled, in progress, completed) */,
  "production_line" TEXT /* Production line used for manufacturing */,
  "shift" TEXT /* Shift during which the production run takes place */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_schedule_rim_id FOREIGN KEY ("rim_id") REFERENCES rim_specifications ("rim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_control" (
  "qc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality control entry */,
  "rim_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the rim inspected */,
  "inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of inspection */,
  "inspector_id" INTEGER /* Identifier of the inspector */,
  "pass_fail" TEXT /* Pass/fail result of the inspection */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments */,
  "defect_code" TEXT /* Code for any defects found during inspection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("qc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_control_defect_code FOREIGN KEY ("defect_code") REFERENCES defect_codes ("defect_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_control_inspector_id FOREIGN KEY ("inspector_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_control_rim_id FOREIGN KEY ("rim_id") REFERENCES rim_specifications ("rim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, production manager, quality inspector) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the user belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_control" (
  "acl_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control entry */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role for which the access control applies */,
  "resource" TEXT /* Resource being accessed (e.g., rim_specifications, production_schedule) */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission level (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  "module" TEXT /* Module to which the access control applies (e.g., Production, Quality Control) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("acl_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES users ("role")
)

CREATE TABLE "defect_codes" (
  "defect_code" TEXT /* Unique defect code */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the defect */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("defect_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "rim_images" (
  "image_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rim image */,
  "rim_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the rim design */,
  "image_data" TEXT /* Binary image data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("image_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rim_images_rim_id FOREIGN KEY ("rim_id") REFERENCES rim_specifications ("rim_id")
)
column_names: Type of material (e.g., steel, welding material)
Cost per unit of the material
Unique identifier for each production schedule entry
Reference to the rim being produced
Start date of the production run
End date of the production run
Quantity of rims to be produced
Status of the production run (e.g., scheduled, in progress, completed)
Shift during which the production run takes place
Unique identifier for each quality control entry
Code for any defects found during inspection
Role of the user (e.g., admin, production manager, quality inspector)
Department to which the user belongs
Unique identifier for each access control entry
Role for which the access control applies
Module to which the access control applies (e.g., Production, Quality Control)
Unique identifier for each rim image
